tools.name: batches_create-batch
tools.description: Create a batch containing multiple labels. Returns the new CreateBatchResponseBody.

tools.name: batches_delete-batch
tools.description: Delete a batch based on its batch id. Sets its status to 'archived'. Required: batch_id. Destructive.

tools.name: batches_get-batch-by-external-id
tools.description: Retreive a batch using an external batch ID. Required: external_batch_id. Returns the GetBatchByExternalIdResponseBody.

tools.name: batches_get-batch-by-id
tools.description: Get batch details for a specific batch id. Required: batch_id. Returns the GetBatchByIdResponseBody.

tools.name: batches_list
tools.description: List the batches associated with your ShipStation account. Optional: status, page (default: 1), page_size (default: 25) (plus 3 more). Returns the ListBatchesResponseBody.

params.name: page
params.description: Return a specific page of results.

params.name: page_size
params.description: The number of results to return per response.

params.name: sort_dir
params.description: Controls the sort order of the query.

tools.name: batches_update-batch
tools.description: Update a batch by id setting its status to 'archived'. Required: batch_id.

tools.name: batches_add_to-batch
tools.description: Add a shipment or rate to a batch. Required: batch_id. Optional: batch_notes, external_batch_id, process_labels (plus 2 more).

params.name: batch_notes
params.description: Add custom messages for a particular batch

params.name: external_batch_id
params.description: A string that uniquely identifies the external batch

params.name: process_labels
params.description: The information used to process the batch

params.name: rate_ids
params.description: Array of rate IDs used in the batch

params.name: shipment_ids
params.description: Array of shipment IDs used in the batch

tools.name: batches_errors_list-batch
tools.description: Errors in batches must be handled differently from synchronous requests. You must retrieve the status of your batch by getting a batch and getting an overview of the statuses or by listing the batch errors. Required: batch_id. Optional: page (default: 1), pagesize. Returns the ListBatchErrorsResponseBody.

params.name: page
params.description: Return a specific page of results.

tools.name: batches_process_batch
tools.description: Create and purchase the labels for the shipments included in the batch. Required: batch_id. Optional: display_scheme, label_format, label_layout (default: 4x6) (plus 1 more).

params.name: display_scheme
params.description: The display format that the label should be shown in.

params.name: label_format
params.description: The possible file formats in which shipping labels can be downloaded.

params.name: ship_date
params.description: The Ship date the batch is being processed for

tools.name: batches_remove_from-batch
tools.description: Remove specific shipment ids or rate ids from a batch. Required: batch_id. Optional: rate_ids, shipment_ids.

params.name: rate_ids
params.description: Array of Rate IDs to be modifed on the batch

params.name: shipment_ids
params.description: The Shipment Ids to be modified on the batch

tools.name: carriers_get-by-id
tools.description: Retrive details about a specific carrier by its carrier id. Required: carrier_id. Returns the GetCarrierByIdResponseBody.

tools.name: carriers_list
tools.description: List all carriers that have been added to this account. Returns the GetCarriersResponseBody.

tools.name: carriers_options_get-carrier
tools.description: Get a list of the options available for a specific carriers. Required: carrier_id. Returns the GetCarrierOptionsResponseBody.

tools.name: carriers_packages_list-carrier-types
tools.description: List the package types associated with a specific carrier. Required: carrier_id. Returns the ListCarrierPackageTypesResponseBody.

tools.name: carriers_services_list-carrier
tools.description: List the services associated with a specific carrier id. Required: carrier_id. Returns the ListCarrierServicesResponseBody.

tools.name: downloads_file
tools.description: Download labels and other shipment-related documents. Required: subdir, filename, dir. Optional: download, rotation.

tools.name: environment_create-webhook
tools.description: Create a webhook for specific events in the environment. Required: event, name, url. Optional: headers, store_id. Returns the new CreateWebhookResponseBody.

params.name: url
params.description: The url that the webhook sends the request to

tools.name: environment_delete-webhook
tools.description: Delete webhook by id. Required: webhook_id. Destructive.

tools.name: environment_get-webhook-by-id
tools.description: Retrieve individual webhook by an ID. Required: webhook_id. Returns the GetWebhookByIdResponseBody.

tools.name: environment_list-webhooks
tools.description: List all webhooks currently enabled for the account. Returns array of EnvironmentListWebhooksItem.

tools.name: environment_update-webhook
tools.description: Update the webhook url property. Required: webhook_id. Optional: headers, name, url.

params.name: url
params.description: The url that the webhook sends the request

tools.name: fulfillments_create
tools.description: Create one or more fulfillments by marking shipments as shipped with tracking information. This will notify customers and marketplaces according to your configuration. Required: fulfillments. Returns the new FulfillmentsCreateResponse.

tools.name: fulfillments_list
tools.description: Retrieve a list of fulfillments based on various filter criteria. You can filter by shipment details, tracking information, dates, and more to find the specific fulfillments you need. Optional: ship_to_name, ship_to_country_code, shipment_number (plus 14 more). Returns the FulfillmentsListResponse.

params.name: ship_to_country_code
params.description: Filter by ship-to country code (2-letter ISO country code)

params.name: order_source_id
params.description: Filter by order source id (store id)

tools.name: inventory_get-levels
tools.description: List SKU inventory levels. Optional: sku, inventory_warehouse_id, inventory_location_id (plus 2 more). Returns the InventoryGetLevelsResponse.

params.name: sku
params.description: Optional list of SKUs to filter down to

params.name: group_by
params.description: Get counts for SKUs across locations or warehouses

tools.name: inventory_update-skustock-levels
tools.description: Update SKU stock levels and related properties. Required: inventory_location_id, quantity, sku, transaction_type. Optional: condition, cost, effective_at (plus 8 more).

params.name: new_condition
params.description: Used with the modify transaction type to change the condition of existing inventory

params.name: new_cost
params.description: Used with the modify transaction type to update the cost of existing inventory

params.name: new_inventory_location_id
params.description: Used with the modify transaction type to move inventory to a new location

params.name: transaction_type
params.description: The type of update to perform: increment: Increase inventory quantity for specified location. decrement: Decreate inventory quantity for specified location given a set of filters. adjust: Adjust on hand quantity of inventory for specified location to the quantity indicated. This is done by either adding or removing inventory in the condition specified. modify: Modify attributes on specified inventory. The filters work the same as for decrement transaction type.

tools.name: inventory-locations_create
tools.description: Create a new inventory location. Required: inventory_warehouse_id, name. Optional: barcode. Returns the new InventoryLocationsCreateResponse.

params.name: barcode
params.description: Physical barcode label for warehouse location bins/shelves used during pick/pack operations. Optional field that accepts any string format.

tools.name: inventory-locations_delete-by-id
tools.description: Delete an inventory location. Required: inventory_location_id. Optional: remove_inventory. Destructive.

params.name: remove_inventory
params.description: If 1, remove all inventory from the location before deleting it. If 0 or missing and the location has On Hand inventory, the request will fail.

tools.name: inventory-locations_get-by-id
tools.description: Get inventory location by ID. Required: inventory_location_id. Returns the InventoryLocationsGetByIdResponse.

tools.name: inventory-locations_list
tools.description: List all inventory locations. Optional: page_size. Returns the InventoryLocationsListResponse.

tools.name: inventory-locations_update
tools.description: Update an inventory location. Required: inventory_location_id. Optional: barcode, name.

params.name: barcode
params.description: Physical barcode label for warehouse location bins/shelves used during pick/pack operations. Optional field that accepts any string format.

tools.name: inventory-warehouses_add-new
tools.description: Create a new inventory warehouse. Required: name. Returns the new InventoryWarehousesAddNewResponse.

tools.name: inventory-warehouses_delete
tools.description: Delete an inventory warehouse. Required: inventory_warehouse_id. Optional: remove_inventory. Destructive.

params.name: remove_inventory
params.description: If 1, remove all inventory from the warehouse before deleting it. If 0 or missing and the warehouse has On Hand inventory, the request will fail.

tools.name: inventory-warehouses_get
tools.description: List all inventory warehouses. Optional: page_size. Returns the InventoryWarehousesGetResponse.

tools.name: inventory-warehouses_get-by-id
tools.description: Get a specific inventory warehouse and related properties using its warehouse ID. Required: inventory_warehouse_id. Returns the InventoryWarehousesGetByIdResponse.

tools.name: inventory-warehouses_update
tools.description: Update an inventory warehouse name. Required: inventory_warehouse_id. Optional: name.

tools.name: labels_create
tools.description: Purchase and print a label for shipment. Required: shipment. Optional: alternative_identifiers, batch_id, carrier_code (plus 44 more). Returns the new CreateLabelResponseBody.

params.name: alternative_identifiers
params.description: Additional information some carriers may provide by which to identify a given label in their system.

params.name: batch_id
params.description: If this label was created as part of a [batch], then this is the unique ID of that batch.

params.name: carrier_code
params.description: The [shipping carrier] who will ship the package, such as `fedex`, `dhl_express`, `stamps_com`, etc.

params.name: carrier_id
params.description: The unique ID of the [carrier account] that was used to create this label

params.name: confirmation
params.description: The type of delivery confirmation that is required for this shipment.

params.name: created_at
params.description: The date and time that the label was created in ShipStation .

params.name: display_scheme
params.description: The display format that the label should be shown in.

params.name: external_order_id
params.description: ID that the Order Source assigned

params.name: external_shipment_id
params.description: A unique user-defined key to identify a shipment. This can be used to retrieve the shipment. > **Warning:** The `external_shipment_id` is limited to 50 characters. Any additional characters will be truncated.

params.name: form_download
params.description: The link to download the customs form (a.k.a. commercial invoice) for this shipment, if any. Forms are in PDF format. This field is null if the shipment does not require a customs form, or if the carrier does not support it.

params.name: insurance_claim
params.description: The link to submit an insurance claim for the shipment. This field is null if the shipment is not insured or if the insurance provider does not support online claim submission.

params.name: insurance_cost
params.description: The insurance cost for this package. Add this to the `shipment_cost` field to get the total cost.

params.name: is_international
params.description: Indicates whether this is an international shipment. That is, the originating country and destination country are different.

params.name: is_return_label
params.description: Indicates whether this is a return label. You may also want to set the `rma_number` so you know what is being returned.

params.name: label_download
params.description: Reference to the various downloadable file formats for the generated label

params.name: label_download_type
params.description: There are two different ways to [download a label]: |Label Download Type | Description...

params.name: label_format
params.description: The file format that you want the label to be in. We recommend `pdf` format because it is supported by all carriers, whereas some carriers do not support the `png` or `zpl` formats.

params.name: label_id
params.description: A string that uniquely identifies the label. This ID is generated by ShipStation when the label is created.

params.name: label_image_id
params.description: The label image resource that was used to create a custom label image.

params.name: label_layout
params.description: The layout (size) that you want the label to be in. The `label_format` determines which sizes are allowed. `4x6` is supported for all label formats, whereas `letter` (8.5' x 11') is only supported for `pdf` format.

params.name: outbound_label_id
params.description: The `label_id` of the original (outgoing) label that the return label is for. This associates the two labels together, which is required by some carriers.

params.name: package_code
params.description: The [package type], such as `thick_envelope`, `small_flat_rate_box`, `large_package`, etc. The code `package` indicates a custom or unknown package type.

params.name: packages
params.description: The label's package(s). > **Note:** Some carriers only allow one package per label. If you attempt to create a multi-package label for a carrier that doesn't allow it, an error will be returned.

params.name: paperless_download
params.description: The paperless details which may contain elements like `href`, `instructions` and `handoff_code`.

params.name: qr_code_download
params.description: The QR code download for the package

params.name: refund_details
params.description: Information about the Refund Assist request for this label. This field is `null` if the label is not eligible for Refund Assist.

params.name: requested_comparison_amount
params.description: The total shipping cost for the specified comparison_rate_type.

params.name: rma_number
params.description: An optional Return Merchandise Authorization number. This field is useful for return labels. You can set it to any string value.

params.name: service_code
params.description: The [carrier service] used to ship the package, such as `fedex_ground`, `usps_first_class_mail`, `flat_rate_envelope`, etc.

params.name: ship_date
params.description: The date that the package was (or will be) shippped. ShipStation will take the day of week into consideration. For example, if the carrier does not operate on Sundays, then a package that would have shipped on Sunday will ship on Monday instead.

params.name: ship_from_service_point_id
params.description: A unique identifier for a carrier drop off point where a merchant plans to deliver packages. This will take precedence over a shipment's ship from address.

params.name: ship_to_service_point_id
params.description: A unique identifier for a carrier service point where the shipment will be delivered by the carrier. This will take precedence over a shipment's ship to address.

params.name: shipment
params.description: The shipment information used to generate the label

params.name: shipment_cost
params.description: The cost of shipping, delivery confirmation, and other carrier charges. This amount **does not** include insurance costs.

params.name: shipment_id
params.description: The shipment that this label is for. ShipStation can create a shipment for you automatically when you [create a label], or you can [create your own shipment] and then [use it to print a label]

params.name: status
params.description: The possible statuses that a [shipping label] can be in. |Status |Description |:------------|:----------------------------------------------------- |`processing` |When labels are created in a [batch], it may take a few minutes for all of the labels in the batch to be created. During this period, they will be in `processing` status. |`completed` |The label was successfully created |`error` |The label could not be created due to an error, such as an invalid delivery address |`voided` |The label has been [voided]

params.name: test_label
params.description: Indicate if this label is being used only for testing purposes.

params.name: trackable
params.description: Indicates whether the shipment is trackable, in which case the `tracking_status` field will reflect the current status and each package will have a `tracking_number`.

params.name: tracking_number
params.description: The tracking number for the package. Tracking number formats vary across carriers.

params.name: tracking_status
params.description: The current status of the package, such as `in_transit` or `delivered`

params.name: tracking_url
params.description: The URL to track the package. This URL is provided by the carrier and is unique to the tracking number.

params.name: void_type
params.description: Indicates how the label was voided. This field is `null` if the label has not been voided.

params.name: voided
params.description: Indicates whether the label has been [voided]

params.name: voided_at
params.description: The date and time that the label was [voided] , or `null` if the label has not been voided

tools.name: labels_create-from-rate
tools.description: When retrieving rates for shipments using the `/rates` endpoint, the returned information contains a `rate_id` property that can be used to generate a label without having to refill in the shipment information repeatedly. Required: rate_id. Optional: display_scheme, label_download_type, label_format (plus 2 more). Returns the new CreateLabelFromRateResponseBody.

params.name: display_scheme
params.description: The display format that the label should be shown in.

params.name: label_download_type
params.description: There are two different ways to [download a label]: |Label Download Type | Description...

params.name: label_format
params.description: The possible file formats in which shipping labels can be downloaded.

params.name: label_layout
params.description: The available layouts (sizes) in which shipping labels can be downloaded. The label format determines which sizes are supported. `4x6` is supported for all label formats, whereas `letter` (8.5' x 11') is only supported for `pdf` format.

tools.name: labels_create-from-rate-shopper
tools.description: Create a label using Rate Shopper to automatically select the best carrier and service based on the specified strategy. For more information about Rate Shopper strategies and use cases, see [Rate Shopping](/rate-shopping#automatic-label-creation-with-rate-shopper). Required: rate_shopper_id (default: cheapest), shipment. Optional: display_scheme (default: label), label_download_type (default: url), label_format (default: pdf) (plus 1 more). Returns the new CreateLabelRateShopperResponseBody.

params.name: rate_shopper_id
params.description: The rate selection strategy. Determines which carrier and service will be automatically selected: - cheapest: Lowest cost option - fastest: Fastest option at the lowest price - best_value: Lowest cost option arriving within 4 days

params.name: label_download_type
params.description: Specifies how the label should be downloaded. - url: Returns URLs to download the label - inline: Returns the label data inline in the response

params.name: shipment
params.description: Shipment details. Must NOT include carrier_id, service_code, or shipping_rule_id as Rate Shopper selects these automatically.

tools.name: labels_create-from-shipment
tools.description: Purchase a label using a shipment ID that has already been created with the desired address and package info. Required: shipment_id. Optional: display_scheme, label_download_type, label_format (plus 2 more). Returns the new CreateLabelFromShipmentResponseBody.

params.name: display_scheme
params.description: The display format that the label should be shown in.

params.name: label_download_type
params.description: There are two different ways to [download a label]: |Label Download Type | Description...

params.name: label_format
params.description: The possible file formats in which shipping labels can be downloaded.

params.name: label_layout
params.description: The available layouts (sizes) in which shipping labels can be downloaded. The label format determines which sizes are supported. `4x6` is supported for all label formats, whereas `letter` (8.5' x 11') is only supported for `pdf` format.

tools.name: labels_get-by-external-shipment-id
tools.description: Find a label by using the external shipment id that was used during label creation. > **Warning:** This endpoint returns only the first label found with the specified `external_shipment_id`. If multiple labels share the same `external_shipment_id`, only the earliest created label will be returned. To retrieve all labels with a specific `external_shipment_id`, use the [list labels endpoint](#operation/list_labels) with the `external_shipment_id` query parameter. Required: external_shipment_id. Optional: label_download_type.

tools.name: labels_get-by-id
tools.description: Retrieve a specific label by its label id. Required: label_id. Optional: label_download_type. Returns the GetLabelByIdResponseBody.

tools.name: labels_list
tools.description: This method returns a list of labels that you've created. You can optionally filter the results as well as control their sort order and the number of results returned at a time. By default all labels are returned 25 at a time, starting with the most recently created ones. You can combine multiple filter options to narrow-down the results. For example, if you only want your UPS labels for your east coast warehouse you could query by both `warehouse_id` and `carrier_id`. Optional: label_status, service_code, carrier_id (plus 13 more).

params.name: label_status
params.description: Only return labels that are currently in the specified status.

params.name: service_code
params.description: Only return labels for a specific carrier service.

params.name: tracking_number
params.description: The tracking number associated with a shipment

params.name: external_shipment_id
params.description: Filter labels by external shipment ID.

params.name: created_at_start
params.description: Used to create a filter for when a resource was created (ex.

params.name: created_at_end
params.description: Used to create a filter for when a resource was created, (ex.

params.name: page
params.description: Return a specific page of results.

params.name: page_size
params.description: The number of results to return per response.

params.name: sort_dir
params.description: Controls the sort order of the query.

params.name: sort_by
params.description: Controls which field the query is sorted by.

tools.name: labels_cancel-refund_cancel-label-refund
tools.description: Cancel a scheduled refund request for a label. Only labels with refund status 'request_scheduled' can be excluded from an upcoming refund request. Required: label_id. Returns the new Label.

tools.name: labels_return_create-label
tools.description: Create a return label for a previously created outbound label. The return label will automatically swap the ship to and ship from addresses from the original label. params.description: The file format that you want the label to be in. We recommend `pdf` format because it is supported by all carriers, whereas some carriers do not support the `png` or `zpl` formats.

params.name: label_image_id
params.description: The label image resource that was used to create a custom label image.

params.name: label_layout
params.description: The layout (size) that you want the label to be in. The `label_format` determines which sizes are allowed. `4x6` is supported for all label formats, whereas `letter` (8.5' x 11') is only supported for `pdf` format.

tools.name: labels_track_get-tracking-log-from-label

tools.name: labels_void_label
tools.description: Void a specific label using its label id. For labels that are paid for at time of label creation, this will also request a refund from the carrier. Required: label_id. Returns the updated VoidLabelResponseBody.

Required: label_layout, shipments. Optional: charge_event, display_scheme, is_return_label (default: false) (plus 9 more). Returns the new MultiLabelResponseBody.

params.description: Indicate if this label is being used only for testing purposes.

tools.name: manifests_create
tools.description: Each ShipStation manifest is created for a specific warehouse, so you'll need to provide the warehouse_id rather than the ship_from address. You can create a warehouse for each location that you want to create manifests for. Returns the new CreateManifestResponseBody.

tools.name: packages_create-type
tools.description: Create a custom package type to better assist in getting accurate rate estimates. Required: name, package_code. Optional: description, dimensions, package_id. Returns the new CreatePackageTypeResponseBody.

params.name: description
params.description: Provides a helpful description for the custom package.

params.name: dimensions
params.description: The custom dimensions for the package.

params.name: package_code
params.description: A [package type] , such as `thick_envelope`, `small_flat_rate_box`, `large_package`, etc. Use the code `package` for custom or unknown package types.

params.name: package_id
params.description: A string that uniquely identifies the package.

tools.name: packages_list-types

params.name: description
params.description: Provides a helpful description for the custom package.

params.name: dimensions

params.name: package_code
params.description: A [package type] , such as `thick_envelope`, `small_flat_rate_box`, `large_package`, etc. Use the code `package` for custom or unknown package types.

tools.name: pickups_list-scheduled

tools.name: pickups_schedule
tools.description: Schedule a package pickup with a carrier. Required: contact_details, label_ids, pickup_window. Optional: cancelled_at, carrier_id, confirmation_number (plus 6 more). Returns the new SchedulePickupResponseBody.

params.name: cancelled_at
params.description: The date and time that the pickup was cancelled in ShipStation .

params.name: carrier_id
params.description: The carrier_id associated with the pickup

params.name: confirmation_number
params.description: The carrier confirmation number for the scheduled pickup.

params.name: created_at
params.description: The date and time that the pickup was created in ShipStation .

params.name: label_ids
params.description: Label IDs that will be included in the pickup request

params.name: pickup_address
params.description: Any residential or business mailing address, anywhere in the world. > **Note:** Either `name` or `company_name` must be set. Both may be specified, if relevant.

params.name: pickup_notes
params.description: Used by some carriers to give special instructions for a package pickup

params.name: pickup_window
params.description: The desired time range for the package pickup.

params.name: pickup_windows
params.description: An array of available pickup windows. Carriers can return multiple times that they will pickup packages.

params.name: warehouse_id
params.description: The warehouse_id associated with the pickup

tools.name: products_list
tools.description: List products. Optional: sku, name, active (plus 2 more). Returns the ProductsListResponse.

params.name: page_size
params.description: The number of results to return per page

tools.name: purchase-orders_create
tools.description: Create a new purchase order with products from a supplier. Required: products, supplier_id, warehouse_id. Optional: currency_code (default: USD), expected_delivery_date, note_to_supplier (plus 6 more). Returns the new PurchaseOrdersCreateResponse.

params.name: note_to_supplier
params.description: Note to include for the supplier

params.name: order_date
params.description: Date when the order was placed

params.name: products
params.description: List of products in the purchase order

tools.name: purchase-orders_get
tools.description: Retrieve detailed information about a specific purchase order including all products. Required: purchase_order_id. Returns the PurchaseOrdersGetResponse.

tools.name: purchase-orders_list
tools.description: Retrieve a paginated list of purchase orders with optional filtering by status, warehouse, dates, and other criteria. Optional: order_number, status, warehouse_id (plus 3 more). Returns the PurchaseOrdersListResponse.

tools.name: purchase-orders_update
tools.description: Update an existing purchase order. Editing limitations: - In the `draft` status, all fields can be edited. - Once a purchase order moves to any other status (open, receiving, received, cancelled, or closed), updating via PUT is no longer allowed. - For purchase orders in `open` status, use the POST /v2/purchase_orders/{purchase_order_id}/shipping_details endpoint to update shipping-related details. Required: purchase_order_id, products, supplier_id, warehouse_id. Optional: currency_code, expected_delivery_date, note_to_supplier (plus 6 more). Returns the updated PurchaseOrdersUpdateResponse.

tools.name: purchase-orders_documents_get-purchase-order-received-summary-pdf
tools.description: Download a PDF document containing the received products summary. Available for purchase orders with status: receiving, received, or closed. Required: purchase_order_id.

tools.name: purchase-orders_documents_get-purchase-order-summary-pdf
tools.description: Download a PDF document containing the purchase order summary. Available for purchase orders with status: open, receiving, received, or closed. Required: purchase_order_id.

tools.name: purchase-orders_receives_purchase-order-products
tools.description: Record receipt of products from a purchase order. This updates inventory levels and may automatically change purchase order status. Required: purchase_order_id, product_receives. Returns the new ReceivesPurchaseOrderProductsResponse.

tools.name: purchase-orders_shipping-details_update-purchase-order
tools.description: Update shipping-related details for a purchase order in `open` status. Editing limitations: - Once a purchase order moves to any higher status (receiving, received, cancelled, or closed), updating shipping details is no longer allowed. - Only the following fields can be modified: shipping_carrier, payment_terms, payment_status, order_date, expected_delivery_date, note_to_supplier, tracking_number, currency_code, and reference_number. Required: purchase_order_id. Optional: currency_code, expected_delivery_date, note_to_supplier (plus 6 more). Returns the new ShippingDetailsUpdatePurchaseOrderResponse.

tools.name: purchase-orders_status_update-purchase-order
tools.description: Update the status of a purchase order. Valid status transitions must be followed. Required: purchase_order_id, status. Returns the new StatusUpdatePurchaseOrderResponse.

params.name: status
params.description: New status for the purchase order

tools.name: rates_calculate
tools.description: It's not uncommon that you want to give your customer the choice between whether they want to ship the fastest, cheapest, or the most trusted route. Most companies don't solely ship things using a single shipping option; so we provide functionality to show you all your options! Returns the new CalculateRatesResponseBody.

tools.name: rates_estimate
tools.description: Get Rate Estimates. Returns array of RatesEstimateItem.

tools.name: rates_get-by-id
tools.description: Retrieve a previously queried rate by its ID. Required: rate_id. Returns the GetRateByIdResponseBody.

tools.name: shipments_assign-user-to
tools.description: Assigns a user to one or more shipments. You can assign a single user to up to 500 shipments at once. Required: shipment_ids, user_id. Returns the new AssignUserResponseBody.

params.name: shipment_ids
params.description: Array of shipment IDs to assign the user to

params.name: user_id
params.description: The ID of the user to assign to the shipments

tools.name: shipments_create
tools.description: Create one or more shipments. Required: shipments. Returns the new CreateShipmentsResponseBody.

tools.name: shipments_get-by-external-id
tools.description: Query Shipments created using your own custom ID convention using this endpoint. Required: external_shipment_id. Returns the GetShipmentByExternalIdResponseBody.

tools.name: shipments_get-by-id
tools.description: Get an individual shipment based on its ID. Required: shipment_id. Returns the GetShipmentByIdResponseBody.

tools.name: shipments_list
tools.description: Get list of Shipments. Required: pickup_id. Optional: shipment_status, batch_id, created_at_start (plus 15 more). Returns the ListShipmentsResponseBody.

params.description: Used to create a filter for when a resource was created, (ex.

params.description: Return a specific page of results.

params.name: page_size
params.description: The number of results to return per response.

params.name: sort_dir
params.description: Controls the sort order of the query.

params.name: shipment_number
params.description: The user or order source defined shipment number

params.name: ship_to_name
params.description: The name associated with the ship_to

params.name: item_keyword
params.description: Returns shipments that contain items that match the specified keyword. Fields searched are Sku, Description, and Options.

params.name: payment_date_start
params.description: Used to create a filter for resources based on the payment_date parameter after a certain time.

params.name: payment_date_end
params.description: Used to create a filter for resources based on the payment_date parameter before a certain time.

params.name: external_shipment_id
params.description: Filter labels by external shipment ID.

tools.name: shipments_update
tools.description: Update an existing shipment's details including addresses, package information, carrier service, and shipping options. Use this endpoint to modify shipment information before purchasing a label. Note: The following fields are read-only and cannot be updated: shipment_id, created_at, modified_at, shipment_status, tags, and total_weight. Required: shipment_id. Optional: advanced_options, amount_paid, assigned_user (plus 41 more). Returns the updated Shipment.

params.name: advanced_options
params.description: Advanced shipment options. These are entirely optional.

params.name: amount_paid
params.description: Total amount paid for the order

params.name: carrier_id
params.description: The carrier account that is billed for the shipping charges

params.name: comparison_rate_type
params.description: Calculate a rate for this shipment with the requested carrier using a ratecard that differs from the default. Only supported for UPS and USPS.

params.name: confirmation
params.description: The type of delivery confirmation that is required for this shipment

params.name: created_at
params.description: The date and time that the shipment was created in ShipStation .

params.name: customs
params.description: Customs information. This is usually only needed for international shipments.

params.name: deliver_by_date
params.description: Date by which the shipment should be delivered

params.name: external_order_id
params.description: ID that the Order Source assigned

params.name: external_shipment_id
params.description: A unique user-defined key to identify a shipment. This can be used to retrieve the shipment. > **Warning:** The `external_shipment_id` is limited to 50 characters. Any additional characters will be truncated.

params.name: hold_until_date
params.description: Date to hold the shipment until

params.name: insurance_provider
params.description: The insurance provider to use for any insured packages in the shipment.

params.name: is_gift
params.description: Indicates if the shipment is a gift

params.name: is_return
params.description: An optional indicator if the shipment is intended to be a return. Defaults to false if not provided.

params.name: items
params.description: Describe the packages included in this shipment as related to potential metadata that was imported from external order sources

params.name: modified_at
params.description: The date and time that the shipment was created or last modified.

params.name: order_source_code
params.description: The order sources that are supported by ShipStation

params.name: packages
params.description: The packages in the shipment. > **Note:** Some carriers only allow one package per shipment. If you attempt to create a multi-package shipment for a carrier that doesn't allow it, an error will be returned.

params.name: retail_rate
params.description: The retail rate for the shipment

params.name: return_to
params.description: The return address for this shipment. Defaults to the `ship_from` address. Can be sent with a warehouse_id, in which case the return address can be overridden. This allows you to have a different address printed on the label while maintaining the origin address.

params.name: service_code
params.description: The [carrier service] used to ship the package, such as `fedex_ground`, `usps_first_class_mail`, `flat_rate_envelope`, etc.

params.name: ship_by_date
params.description: Date by which the shipment should be shipped

params.name: ship_date
params.description: The date that the shipment was (or will be) shippped. ShipStation will take the day of week into consideration. For example, if the carrier does not operate on Sundays, then a package that would have shipped on Sunday will ship on Monday instead.

params.name: ship_from
params.description: The shipment's origin address. If you frequently ship from the same location, consider [creating a warehouse]. Then you can simply specify the `warehouse_id` rather than the complete address each time.

params.name: shipment_number
params.description: A non-unique user-defined number used to identify a shipment. If undefined, this will match the external_shipment_id of the shipment. > **Warning:** The `shipment_number` is limited to 50 characters. Any additional characters will be truncated.

params.name: shipment_status
params.description: The current status of the shipment

params.name: shipping_rule_id
params.description: ID of the shipping rule, which you want to use to automate carrier/carrier service selection for the shipment

params.name: store_id
params.description: The store ID associated with the shipment

params.name: tags
params.description: Arbitrary tags associated with this shipment. Tags can be used to categorize shipments, and shipments can be queried by their tags. Note: Tags require object structure with name property, not simple strings.

params.name: total_weight
params.description: The combined weight of all packages in the shipment

params.name: validate_address
params.description: Address validation option for ship_to and ship_from addresses

params.name: warehouse_id
params.description: The [warehouse] that the shipment is being shipped from. Either `warehouse_id` or `ship_from` must be specified.

tools.name: shipments_cancel_shipments
tools.description: Mark a shipment cancelled, if it is no longer needed or being used by your organized. Any label associated with the shipment needs to be voided first An example use case would be if a batch label creation job is going to run at a set time and only queries `pending` shipments. Marking a shipment as cancelled would remove it from this process. Required: shipment_id.

tools.name: shipments_internal-notes_update-shipment
tools.description: Update the internal notes on a shipment. You can either overwrite the existing notes entirely or append new text to the current notes. | Mode | `note` value | Result | |------|-------------|--------| | `overwrite` | `'new note'` | Replaces existing notes | | `overwrite` | `''` or `' '` | Clears the note | | `append` | `'new note'` | Appends text to existing notes |. Required: shipment_id, mode, note. Returns the new InternalNotesUpdateShipmentResponse.

params.name: mode
params.description: Controls how the note is applied. - `append` adds the text to the end of any existing notes. - `overwrite` replaces the existing notes entirely (use an empty string to clear).

params.name: note
params.description: The note text to set or append.

tools.name: shipments_rates_list-shipment
tools.description: Get Rates for the shipment information associated with the shipment ID. Required: shipment_id. Optional: created_at_start. Returns the ListShipmentRatesResponseBody.

params.name: created_at_start
params.description: Used to create a filter for when a resource was created (ex.

tools.name: shipments_tags_untag-shipment
tools.description: Remove an existing tag from the Shipment object. Required: shipment_id, tag_name. Destructive.

tools.name: totes_create-batch

tools.name: totes_delete
tools.description: Delete a tote by its ID. Required: tote_id. Destructive.

tools.name: totes_get-by-id
tools.description: Retrieve details of a specific tote. Required: tote_id. Returns the Tote.

tools.name: totes_get-quantities
tools.description: Get the number of totes in each warehouse. Returns the TotesGetQuantitiesResponse.

tools.name: totes_list

tools.name: totes_update
tools.description: Update the name or barcode of an existing tote. Required: tote_id, inventory_warehouse_id, tote_name. Optional: tote_barcode.

params.name: inventory_warehouse_id
params.description: The warehouse where this tote is located

tools.name: tracking_stop
tools.description: Unsubscribe from tracking updates for a package. Optional: carrier_code, tracking_number.

params.name: tracking_number
params.description: The tracking number associated with a shipment

tools.name: users_list
tools.description: List users. Optional: status, page_size (default: 25), page (default: 1). Returns the UsersListResponse.

params.name: page_size
params.description: The number of results to return per page

tools.name: warehouses_get-by-id
tools.description: Retrieve warehouse data based on the warehouse ID. Required: warehouse_id. Returns the GetWarehouseByIdResponseBody.

tools.name: warehouses_list
